pain_point_label: Unclear Reporting Thresholds & Victim Retaliation Fears

matches.match_rationale: The pain point is partly driven by absence of clear, written reporting definitions and whistleblower safeguard documentation. Text generation can produce scenario-based reporting threshold guides, anonymised case vignettes, and confidential reporting policy templates tailored to local regulatory frameworks — filling the guidance vacuum staff currently face.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted policy authoring tool that generates plain-language reporting threshold guides and whistleblower protection FAQs, customised per jurisdiction and site, reducing the ambiguity that causes both under- and over-reporting.

matches.match_rationale: Staff incident descriptions submitted via text (emails, welfare logs, free-text forms) can be classified against a taxonomy of reportable vs. non-reportable categories, giving staff an instant threshold signal and reducing the paralysis of individual judgment. This directly addresses the under/over-reporting bifurcation described in the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A real-time incident triage tool where staff type a brief description of a concern and receive an AI-generated classification — 'likely reportable / borderline / not reportable' — with cited policy rationale, removing the burden of solo judgment.

matches.match_rationale: A RAG system grounded in the operator's safeguarding policies, local authority guidance, and child protection legislation can answer staff questions like 'Is physical punishment at home reportable?' with citations — providing authoritative, policy-backed answers rather than relying on memory or supervisory availability. This directly combats the guidance vacuum that causes reporting paralysis.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A confidential safeguarding Q&A assistant for staff that retrieves relevant policy sections and statutory guidance, then synthesises a clear answer with source references — accessible 24/7 without requiring a manager's sign-off before raising a concern.

matches.match_rationale: A multi-turn conversational agent can walk a staff member through a structured safeguarding decision tree — asking clarifying questions about what was observed, who was involved, and contextual factors — before arriving at a threshold determination and recommended next step. This mirrors the guided professional judgment a trained DSL would provide, without requiring immediate human availability.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A confidential 'Reporting Advisor' chatbot embedded in the school's intranet that guides staff through an evidence-based decision process, concludes with a threshold recommendation, and optionally submits an anonymised concern record — reducing both under-reporting due to uncertainty and over-reporting due to anxiety.
